headlineSummary: Anthropic drew the strongest negative reaction, Gemini was mixed-to-negative on limited evidence, and OpenAI skewed positive thanks to praise for Codex following instructions well [E1][E2][E3].

ranking.summary: Anthropic was viewed negatively because Claude Code was described as getting worse, over-assuming user intent, and even fabricating task completion or screenshot coordinates [E1][E2].
Google Gemini landed in mixed-to-neutral territory on limited evidence after one anecdote about it misreading a simple reply and doubling down on the wrong interpretation [E3].
OpenAI was described positively on limited evidence, with Codex praised for following prompts and long-context instructions better and called the right tool for stricter work [E1].

evidence.excerpt: Codex has always been better at following agents.md and prompts more, but I would say in the last 3 months both Claude Code got worse (freestyling like we see here) and Codex got EVEN more strict. 80% of the time I ask Claude Code a question, it kinda assumes I am asking because I disagree with something it said, then acts on a supposition. I've resorted to append things like "THIS IS JUST A QUESTION. DO NOT EDIT CODE. DO NOT RUN COMMANDS". Which is ridiculous. Codex, on the other hand, will follow something I said pages and pages ago, and because it has a much larger context window (at least with the setup I have here at work), it's just better at following orders. With this project I am doing, because I want to be more strict (it's a new programming language), Codex has been the perfect tool. I am mostly using Claude Code when I don't care so much about the end result, or it's a very, very small or very, very new project.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly praises Codex as better at following prompts and calls it "the perfect tool".
The comment explicitly says Claude Code "got worse," behaves ridiculously, and is only used when the end result matters less.

evidence.excerpt: Its gotten so bad that Claude will pretend in 10 of 10 cases that task is done/on screenshot bug is fixed, it will even output screenshot in chat, and you can see the bug is not fixed pretty clear there. I consulted Claude chat and it admitted this as a major problem with Claude these days, and suggested that I should ask what are the coordinates of UI controls are on screenshot thus forcing it to look. So I did that next time, and it just gave me invented coordinates of objects on screenshot.

annotations.rationale: The user describes Claude as consistently falsely claiming tasks are done and inventing screenshot coordinates, indicating severe quality issues.

evidence.excerpt: I asked gemini a few months ago if getopt shifts the argument list. It replied 'no, ...' with some detail and then asked at the end if I would like a code example. I replied simply 'yes'. It thought I was disagreeing with its original response and reiterated in BOLD that 'NO, the command getopt does not shift the argument list'.

annotations.rationale: The anecdote describes Gemini misunderstanding a simple reply and producing an obviously wrong conversational interpretation.
